data.title: AAL, DAL, UAL: Airline Stocks Rise as Analyst Sees Revenue Bucking Historical Trend
data.description:
Shares of leading U.S. carriers American Airlines (AAL), Delta Air Lines (DAL), and United Airlines (UAL) rose about 2% on Thursday morning. This came after BMO Capital Markets (BMO) raised its price targets on the shares. The firm noted that U.S. carriers are set to break a historical trend: maintaining high revenue even as jet fuel costs continue to fall.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

How the U.S.-Iran War Impacted Airlines

Earlier in the year, estimates suggested that airlines could be forced to cough up an extra $11 billion in jet fuel costs in 2026. This estimate emerged as the U.S.-Iran war triggered a surge in energy prices.

The development forced airlines to raise plane ticket prices. This sparked fears that air travel could slow down, hurting carriers' revenues.

Analyst Flags Revenue Retention Capacity

However, BMO Capital Markets analyst Michael Goldie on Wednesday noted that he expects total revenue per available seat mile (TRASM) to remain above historical levels even as jet fuel costs continue to fall. The analyst noted that, historically, TRASM usually declines when jet fuel costs drop. However, he sees capacity for carriers to defy the trend.

Defending the thesis, Goldie noted that demand for air travel has remained high even as fare increases have slowed. He believes that the weaker role of discount carriers, the rollout of more premium cabin services, and earnings beyond aircraft services will help airlines buck the trend.

How BMO Capital Now Rates the Airline Stocks

Based on these factors, Goldie raised the price targets of several airlines. Key highlights include:

  • American Airlines (AAL) — price target raised from $13.50 to $19.59, implying 8% upside from Wednesday's closing price. Hold rating is reaffirmed
  • Delta Air Lines (DAL) — price target lifted from $80 to $105, suggesting about 13% upside from Wednesday's closing price. Buy rating is maintained.
  • United Airlines (UAL) — price target raised from $130 to $157.5, predicting about 17% upside from Wednesday's closing price. Buy rating is reiterated.

Who Is Michael Goldie?

Goldie is a four-star analyst who ranks in the top 22% of the more than 12,000 Wall Street analysts tracked on TipRanks.

He primarily covers the aerospace industry. The analyst boasts a 100% success rate and has generated an average return of 23% for investors based on his ratings.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Nvidia (NVDA) Poaches Microsoft Executive to Lead Its 'Field Operations'
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) has hired a senior executive from Microsoft (MSFT) to lead its field operations.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

NVDS: built for a short position on NVDA

In a statement, Nvidia said that it has hired Nicholas Parker as Executive Vice President of Worldwide Field Operations, effective August 24 of this year. Parker, age 55, joins Nvidia after spending the last 26 years at Microsoft, where he most recently served as Executive Vice President and Chief Business Officer in the Worldwide Sales & Solutions unit.

Parker also led Microsoft's partner sales and solutions teams during his tenure at the technology giant. Typically, field operations involve business activities and services performed away from company's central office. At Nvidia, this includes mobile teams, on-site technicians, and field sales representatives. Nvidia continues to expand sales of its microchips and processors around the world.

Parker's Compensation at Nvidia

Parker's compensation package includes an annual base salary of $1 million, a sign-on bonus of $5 million, and annual variable compensation of $1.5 million. He will also receive Nvidia stock options worth up to $35 million that vest over the next four years.

Parker's appointment follows the announced retirement of Ajay K. Puri, a 21-year Nvidia executive who notified the company of his intention to retire on June 28, 2026. Puri is expected to remain with the company in a senior advisory role during the transition.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Why Is the Stock Market Up Today, 7/2/26?
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) are trading higher on Thursday as investors digest mixed jobs data.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The U.S. economy added 57,000 nonfarm payrolls last month, falling short of the estimate of 110,000. Furthermore, April's and May's payrolls were revised down by a combined 74,000 jobs. At the same time, the unemployment rate eased to 4.2% from 4.3% in May. That was below expectations of 4.3% and also marked a one-year low.

Payrolls Miss Fuels Interest Rate Relief

The nonfarm payrolls miss reduces pressure on the Fed to hike rates. At the next Fed meeting on July 29, the odds of a 25 bps hike dropped to 19.8% from 28.9% a day ago. Lower rates are generally a positive for stocks because they reduce borrowing costs and increase the present value of future earnings. They also encourage investment and growth.

Elsewhere, investors continue to rotate into beaten-down software names. Palantir (PLTR), Fair Isaac (FICO), and Adobe (ADBE) are all well in the green. Furthermore, AI-related stocks are showing mixed price action, highlighted by Intel (INTC), KLA Corp (KLAC), and SanDisk (SNDK).
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: 'Don't Fool Yourself,' Says Top Investor About Micron Stock
data.description:
It's no secret that Micron (NASDAQ:MU) has been one of the AI trade's biggest winners with strong AI-boosted demand for memory amid shortages giving the company enormous pricing power.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

As such, expectations were high heading into the company's recent fiscal third-quarter results. The memory maker hit all the right notes and managed to assuage any fears that the memory boom is about to lose momentum, delivering an estimate-trouncing report and outlook.

However, a top investor with the moniker of Tech Stock Pros (TSP) argues that the results are misleading, mainly because the investor believes the real FY26 upside is not coming from AI-linked HBM (high bandwidth memory), but from traditional DRAM and NAND. That matters, says the 5-star investor, because those parts of the business are still cyclical, rather than benefiting from the structural shift associated with AI. TSP notes that HBM is already sold out for the year with no meaningful volume ramp expected in the near term, and management itself has said non-AI gross margins are actually higher than HBM.

On the surface, Q3 looked very strong. Revenue came in at $41.5 billion (a $5.64 billion beat), up 346% year-over-year and 74% sequentially, and guidance for next quarter points to $50 billion, also ahead of expectations. But the key point is that this growth is almost entirely price-driven. DRAM revenue rose 67% quarter over quarter, but bit shipments only grew 2%, while ASPs (average selling prices) jumped 62%. NAND showed the same pattern, with revenue up sharply but again mostly because of pricing, not volume. In other words, ASPs are "carrying the show."

That shows up even more clearly in margins. Gross margins surged from 56.8% to 84.6% in just a few quarters. That is an extraordinary move for any semiconductor company, let alone a memory business. But the investor's concern is that the momentum is already slowing. The next-quarter guide only implies a small additional increase, which suggests margins may be close to peaking.

Another concern is the new long-term SCAs (strategic customer agreements). Micron has signed deals worth around $100 billion, backed by $22 billion in deposits.

"Now, this sounds great, but the right read is that this is bad because it means management is trying to lock in the current pricing," TSP, who ranks among the top 3% of investors on TipRanks, said on the matter. With take-or-pay structures and unclear enforcement, these contracts could end up capping upside instead of enhancing it, especially if spot prices move more than the contract bands allow.

On the supply side, things are also shifting. SK hynix has started prioritizing general-purpose DRAM over HBM, which signals that tight AI-related supply may be easing. At the same time, Chinese producers are ramping DDR5 output. Both developments suggest that more supply is coming back into the system, which historically pressures pricing in memory markets.

Put all that together and Tech Stock Pros rates MU stock a Strong Sell. It is worth noting, however, that TSP has maintained a Sell rating on Micron since March. During that period, the shares have gained 136%. (To watch TSP's track record, click here)

That bearish take gets no support on Wall Street right now. In fact, based on 29 Buys vs. 1 Hold, the analyst consensus rates the stock a Strong Buy. Going by the $1,563.93 average price target, a year from now shares will be changing hands for a 48% premium. (See Micron stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: EchoStar (ECHO) Stock Jumps on a Dish DBS & Dish Wireless Bankruptcy Filing
data.description:
EchoStar's (ECHO) stock was up after Dish DBS and several of its units, including Dish Wireless, filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy as part of a prepackaged debt restructuring plan. The move followed a deal the company reached with creditors in March. More than 88% of Dish DBS noteholders have agreed to back the plan, which is meant to move through court quickly. The company said it expects to exit Chapter 11 before the end of the third quarter of 2026.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

EchoStar chairman and CEO Charlie Ergen said, "EchoStar has been at the forefront of telecommunications for over 45 years, and these steps will position the business for an even stronger future." The filing is meant to help Dish DBS pay off debt early, improve its balance sheet, and give the company more room for future plans. The filing does not affect EchoStar, Boost Mobile, Gen Mobile, Hughes, Dish TV, or Sling TV. Those units will keep running as normal, and the company said customers, workers, and daily service will not see any changes.

Dish DBS also asked the court for approval to keep paying vendors and other partners during the bankruptcy process. Dish Wireless is also part of the filing as it wraps up the shutdown of its former 5G network. The company said the court process will help sell its remaining assets and settle claims from creditors. EchoStar noted that the filing became necessary after delays in its planned spectrum sale to AT&T (T) left Dish DBS without enough cash to repay $2 billion of notes due on July 1. Once that sale closes, the company expects to repay those notes in full and continue with its restructuring plan.

EchoStar Stock Movement Today

EchoStar investors appear pleased with the prepackaged debt restructuring plan and bankruptcy filing. The company's shares have rallied 1.94% on Thursday, but were down 6.84% year-to-date. However, the shares were still up 221.68% over the past 12 months.

ECHO stock trading activity today saw some 233,000 shares change hands. This is still well below the company's three-month average daily trading volume of about 8.7 million shares.

Is EchoStar Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, the analysts' consensus rating for EchoStar is Moderate Buy, based on two Buy ratings over the past three months. With that comes an average ECHO stock price target of $158, suggesting a possible 53.56% upside for the shares. (See ECHO's Stock Forecast)
data.author: William White

data.title: S&P 500 Rebounds as Unemployment Rate Slips to 1-Year Low
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SPX) opened Thursday on higher ground following a labor market update from the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS).

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

In June, the unemployment rate fell to 4.2% from 4.3% in the prior month. That came in below expectations of 4.3% and also marked the lowest level since June 2025. At the same time, the BLS noted that the labor force participation rate ticked lower by 0.3% to 61.5%. That decline likely helped push the unemployment rate lower, as fewer people were counted as actively seeking work.

Labor Market Holds Firm Despite Weak Payroll Gains

Still, the lower unemployment rate indicates a resilient labor market in light of higher tariff rates and uncertainties stemming from the U.S.-Iran war. In addition, companies have been hesitant to lay off workers for several months. That's been evidenced by initial jobless claims remaining in a tight range since late 2025.

Furthermore, the BLS also said that nonfarm payrolls rose by 57,000 in June, well below the estimate of 110,000. However, the miss has lowered rate hike odds, supporting equities in the process.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Stock Futures Inch Higher; Investors Await June Jobs Report
data.description:
Stock futures edged higher on Thursday morning as investors awaited the June jobs report, which will provide key insights into U.S. employment and influence the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 Index (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.15%, 0.11%, and 0.13%, respectively, at 8:25 a.m. EDT on July 2.

Oil prices trended lower amid easing tensions in the Middle East. At the time of writing, Brent crude (CM:BZ) was down 1.7% to $70.35 per barrel, while WTI crude (CM:CL) fell 1.92% to $67.23.

During Wednesday's regular session, the Nasdaq Composite fell 0.66% while the S&P 500 slipped 0.22% due to sharp declines in chip stocks. Micron (MU) and SanDisk (SNDK) plunged more than 10%, while Intel (INTC) fell over 9%. Meanwhile, the Dow Jones briefly hit a new intraday record before ending little changed.

Meanwhile, Palantir (PLTR) stock was up 3% in Thursday's pre-market trading after D.A. Davidson upgraded the AI-powered data analytics company to Buy from Hold, citing an attractive valuation. Also, AeroVironment (AVAV) stock was up 4% after the defense technology company won a $500 million contract with the U.S. Army to develop counter-drone capabilities.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Tesla Stock Falls Despite Beating Q2 Deliveries; China Sales Rise for Eight Straight Months
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) shares were down 2.9% at the time of writing after briefly rallying as the EV maker beat second quarter delivery expectations by a wide margin. Tesla delivered a total of 480,126 vehicles during the second quarter, while producing 451,758 vehicles over the same period. Meanwhile, analysts were expecting around 406,000 deliveries in Q2.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

TSLQ: built for a short position on TSLA

Q2 deliveries increased by about 25% from roughly 384,000 vehicles in the same quarter last year and were up 34.1% from 358,023 deliveries in the first quarter of 2026.

Tesla's Deliveries Show Solid Rebound

Tesla does not disclose deliveries by region or individual model. However, the company said its Model 3 sedan and Model Y SUV accounted for 467,762 deliveries, or 97% of the total.

Tesla is trying to recover after two straight years of declining vehicle sales. The slowdown has been driven by weaker demand and the expiration of the $7,500 U.S. EV tax credit. Moreover, consumer backlash linked to CEO Elon Musk's political views and involvement with the Trump administration weighed on the brand in some markets.

Tesla China Sales Rise for an Eighth Straight Month

Tesla also reported strong momentum in China, one of the largest markets. According to the China Passenger Car Association (CPCA), Tesla China's wholesale volume rose 24.4% year-over-year to 89,091 autos in June. Sales also increased 3.62% from May's 85,982 units, making June the company's strongest month of 2026 so far.

Moreover, Tesla's EV registrations rose in several European markets. According to regional authorities, Tesla's registrations in June jumped 104.9% in France and 56.3% in Sweden, compared to the prior-year period. Meanwhile, new car registrations rose 39% to 1,778 autos in Denmark but fell 43% year-over-year to 3,222 units in Norway.

Is Tesla a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

Analysts remain divided on Tesla's long-term outlook. On TipRanks, TSLA has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 11 Buys, 14 Holds, and three Sell ratings. The average Tesla price target of $403.66 implies 2.6% downside potential from current levels. Year-to-date, TSLA shares have dropped 7%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Quantum Computing News: IQM Hits Nasdaq as QLEP Raises $230 Million for Quantum Tech Deals
data.description:
Welcome to Thursday's quantum update. The biggest investor-facing theme is that quantum computing is moving further into public markets, national tech policy, sovereign infrastructure, and quantum-safe software. The sector is still early, but the latest news shows more capital formation, more government support, and more companies trying to turn quantum technology into usable commercial platforms.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

Explore QBTX for 2X leverage on QBTS

1. IQM Goes Public Through SPAC Deal

IQM Quantum Computers, a quantum computing company that builds superconducting quantum systems, has completed its business combination with Real Asset Acquisition Corp. and is set to begin trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the symbol IQMX. The deal gives IQM about €198.7 million, or $233.5 million, in net proceeds from the business combination and a private investment.

2. Quantum Leap Raises $230 Million for AI and Quantum Deals

Quantum Leap Acquisition Corp. (QLEP), a newly listed SPAC focused on potential deals in AI, quantum computing, and blockchain, completed a $230 million IPO by selling 23 million units at $10 per unit. The company has not yet named an acquisition target, but its mandate gives it room to pursue a private technology business seeking a public-market path. Quantum Leap's Class A shares and warrants now trade separately on the NYSE under QLEP and QLEP WS, while its units stopped trading under QLEPU.

3. Shanghai Builds a Quantum Computing Hub

Shanghai launched a quantum computing industrial hub in its Xuhui district with 26 founding companies and a package of funding programs and subsidies. The incubation zone will offer up to 100 million yuan, or about $14.73 million, for research, innovation, and shared platforms, plus up to 20 million yuan for companies building their first commercial products. This move adds Shanghai to China's growing race for quantum leadership, alongside hubs such as Hefei, Beijing, and Shenzhen.

4. Archer and IonQ Explore Sovereign Quantum Computing in Australia

Archer Materials Limited (AXE), an Australian quantum technology company, signed a three-year agreement with IonQ Inc. (IONQ). Archer will pay IonQ $1.5 million over the initial term for access to IonQ Forte-class systems, future Tempo-class systems, simulators, and advisory services. The companies will also explore the feasibility of deploying an IonQ quantum computer in Australia. Archer said the work could support use cases across defense, AI, mining, healthcare, and research, while giving Australian customers local access to advanced quantum computing services.

5. BTQ Adds QPerfect's Quantum Emulator

BTQ Technologies Corp. (BTQ), a quantum technology company focused on quantum-safe security and related infrastructure, received final approval from the French foreign investment authority to acquire QPerfect in a fully acquired transaction. The deal adds QPerfect's MIMIQ quantum emulator, digital twin tools, and quantum control capabilities to BTQ's technology stack. The French approval came with commitments to preserve QPerfect's local operations, research, and intellectual property, which highlights how strategic quantum assets are becoming a national-interest issue.

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to line up notable pure-play quantum stocks. It's a great tool for investors to gain a deeper view of each stock and the broader quantum sector.
data.author: Ran Melamed
